_cache_meta.description: Hard-cached CourtListener v4 citation-lookup response for a FABRICATED case. 999 U.S. 9999 is a syntactically-valid U.S. Reports citation (valid reporter 'U.S.', integer volume/page) whose volume is far beyond any published volume (~600 in 2026), so it does not — and for decades will not — exist. The cold open flashes RED: this case does not exist.
_cache_meta.case_facts_source: No case. This is the hallucinated-citation pattern that got real lawyers sanctioned (Mata v. Avianca, 2023): a confident AI cite to a case that is not in any reporter. A clearly out-of-range volume guarantees a deterministic 404 (never an accidental 200 against a real different case).
_cache_meta.envelope_note: Matches the documented v4 citation-lookup schema for a valid-but-not-found citation: status 404, empty clusters. The live overlay (once COURTLISTENER_TOKEN lands) confirms this 404.
